name: Wizard
description: A reusable, generic multi-step wizard component for guiding users through complex processes. Supports both horizontal and vertical layouts with smooth Framer Motion animations, click navigation between steps, custom icons, completed step indicators, and controlled component behavior. Perfect for onboarding flows, multi-step forms, and sequential processes.
usage_guidelines: Use for multi-step forms, onboarding flows, KYC processes, or complex data entry workflows.
Control current step with current prop and handle changes via onChange callback.
Define steps using Wizard.Step compound component pattern.
Provide clear, descriptive titles for each step.
Use customIcon prop for visual step identification.
Choose vertical layout for detailed step content, horizontal for compact navigation.
Handle step validation in parent component before allowing navigation.
Use disabled prop on steps to prevent navigation to incomplete steps.
Provide navigation controls (Next/Previous buttons) in step content.

current.description: Currently active step index (0-based). Controls which step is displayed.

onChange.description: Callback fired when any step indicator is clicked. Receives the clicked step index.

vertical.description: Display steps vertically with inline content. False for horizontal slider layout.

children.description: Wizard.Step components defining each step of the wizard.

className.description: Additional CSS classes for wizard container.

title.description: Step title displayed in the indicator. Can be string or custom React element.

children.description: Step content to display when step is active.

customIcon.description: Custom icon or string for step indicator. Overrides default step number. Completed steps show checkmark.

disabled.description: Disables step navigation. Step appears grayed out and cannot be clicked.

horizontal.description: Default layout with step indicators at top and content below
features: Step indicators in horizontal row with connecting lines
Content slides horizontally like a carousel
Only active step content is visible
Compact design suitable for limited vertical space
Responsive with wrapping on small screens

vertical.description: Vertical layout with steps stacked and content inline
features: Steps stacked vertically with connecting lines
Content appears directly under active step title
best_for: Forms with extensive content per step

active.visual: Primary color indicator, highlighted title, content visible
active.indicator: Step number or custom icon with primary background

completed.visual: Checkmark icon, primary color, connecting line filled

inactive.visual: Gray indicator, normal title, no content
inactive.indicator: Step number or custom icon with gray background

disabled.visual: Grayed out, reduced opacity, not clickable

animations.performance: Uses Framer Motion with transform-based animations for optimal performance

compound_component_pattern.description: Uses compound component pattern with Wizard.Step
compound_component_pattern.usage: Wizard.Step components are children of Wizard component

styling_info.visual_description: Step indicators show as numbered circles (or custom icons) connected by lines. Active step highlighted with primary color. Completed steps show checkmarks. Content area displays current step with smooth transitions. Vertical mode shows steps stacked with inline content. Horizontal mode shows steps in row with content below.
styling_info.responsive: Horizontal indicators wrap on small screens. Vertical layout maintains structure across breakpoints.
styling_info.dark_mode: Full dark mode support with appropriate color adjustments

Do: Use controlled component pattern (manage current state in parent)
Validate step data before allowing navigation to next step
Use disabled prop to prevent navigation to incomplete steps
Provide Next/Previous buttons in step content for navigation
Use custom icons for better visual identification
Save progress at each step for better UX
Provide clear completion feedback on final step
Use vertical layout for complex, detailed steps
Use horizontal layout for simple, quick processes
Dont: Don't use for simple forms (use regular form instead)
Don't allow skipping required steps without validation
Don't forget to handle validation errors
Don't make steps too long or complex (break into smaller steps)
Don't use too many steps (optimal: 3-7 steps)
Don't forget to provide navigation controls
Don't allow navigation to disabled steps
Don't forget to handle browser back button
Don't lose user progress on page refresh (persist state)

integration_notes: Uses Framer Motion for animations (peer dependency)
Controlled component - parent manages current step state
Animations disabled on initial mount for better UX
Full TypeScript support with exported types

recommendations: Provide skip navigation option if appropriate
Announce step changes to screen readers

performance_notes: Uses transform-based animations for optimal performance
Lazy rendering - only active step content is fully rendered in horizontal mode
Memoization opportunities for step content components
Animations can be disabled by modifying animationsEnabled context value
